findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 870 of 870 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged trip.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
findings.what: Some scheduled trips travel implausibly fast between stops.
findings.why: Usually a typo'd stop time; riders get impossible arrival estimates.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stops_match_shape_out_of_order' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Stops match shape out of order (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A few minutes in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Delete the empty calendars or set their service days.
findings.what: Some service calendars have no days of the week switched on.
findings.why: Trips tied to these calendars never run; they are dead data that can mask real schedule problems.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 5 kinds of issue across 398 instances (0 error, 398 warning, 0 informational).

findings.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
findings.what: Service data runs out in 28 day(s).
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data runs out in 28 day(s). Publish an updated feed soon or riders will lose trip planning.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: This agency publishes realtime, but the feed needs an access key we don't have yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: Service data runs out in 28 days; renew to qualify.
States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 100% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. Service data runs out in 28 days; renew to qualify. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 100% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: If you report to the National Transit Database, your GTFS agency_id should be your five-digit NTD ID by report year 2026 (2025 for full reporters). We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this one is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data runs out in 28 days; renew before you certify.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed is close to NTD-ready. Service data runs out in 28 days; renew before you certify.

recommendations.effort: A per-route color setting in your scheduling software.
recommendations.fix: Adjust route_color or route_text_color so each pair clears 4.5:1. Often switching the text between black and white is enough.
recommendations.what: 3 route badge(s) pair a color and text color below the WCAG 4.5:1 contrast bar (510, 520, 555).
recommendations.why: Low-contrast route badges are hard to read for riders with low vision and wash out on a phone screen in sunlight at the stop.

top_fixes.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
top_fixes.what: Service data runs out in 28 day(s).
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

top_fixes.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
top_fixes.what: 870 of 870 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
top_fixes.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'stop_too_far_from_shape_using_user_distance' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Stop too far from shape using user distance (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.
